key_positions: Strongly support the goals of the Paris Agreement to limit global warming to 1.5 degrees Celsius above pre-industrial levels.
Invest heavily in renewable energy sources like solar and wind power through federal tax incentives and direct subsidies.
Promote the development and adoption of electric vehicles through consumer rebates and national charging infrastructure investment.
Pursue a whole-of-government approach to reducing domestic greenhouse gas emissions across all major economic sectors.
Champion public and private investment in clean energy research, development, and commercialisation to drive innovation.
red_lines: Will not agree to binding emissions reduction targets without equivalent commitments from all other major emitting economies.
Opposes unilateral carbon tariffs that could undermine multilateral trade relationships and existing WTO obligations.
Rejects any international framework that imposes disproportionate economic costs on American workers and energy-intensive industries.
